Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RESCUE ROOFING CO.'s Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Rescue Roofing Co.'s motion for determination of good faith settlement is denied without prejudice to its renewal upon a proper evidentiary showing. Although Rescue Roofing asserts this is a "no-liability" case and that "none of Plaintiffs' alleged damages implicate Rescue's narrow scope of work," it has not presented any admissible evidence that would enable the Court to determine "a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v., Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) "If 'there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion.' " (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348 [settling parties did not proffer any evidence regarding their proportionate liability for plaintiff's alleged injury, but merely presented a series of "questionable assumptions" in their memorandum to show the settlement amount was reasonable].) Even if counsel had personal knowledge of such information, counsel's declaration does not provide any such evidence. (See Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 834 [attorney's declaration regarding settling defendant's liability insufficient where he failed to provide specific supporting facts or expert opinion].) While Rescue Roofing is correct that the party asserting the lack of good faith has the burden on that issue (Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d)), the moving party must provide the court in the first instance with a sufficient evidentiary basis upon which to make a good faith determination. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(b) ["The issue of the good faith of a settlement may be determined on the basis of affidavits served with the notice of hearing, and any counteraffidavits filed in response . . . ." (emphasis added)].) Rescue Roofing has not done so.

A Proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
